summary.headline: Best opportunity today: MTD rollout support still leads, with late-payment automation a close second as UK policy pressure rises.
summary.best_opportunity: Making Tax Digital onboarding, cleanup and quarterly-support automation for sole traders, landlords and small accountancy firms
summary.why_now: HMRC's live guidance says Making Tax Digital for Income Tax became mandatory from 6 April 2026 for qualifying income above £50,000, expanding to £30,000 from 6 April 2027 and £20,000 from 6 April 2028. That creates immediate setup work now and recurring quarterly support next.

top_opportunities.name: Making Tax Digital onboarding and quarterly-support automation
top_opportunities.description: Sell a fixed-scope package that checks eligibility, cleans records, connects bookkeeping software, sets reminder workflows and gives quarterly update support for MTD for Income Tax.
top_opportunities.why_now: Evidence is strong. GOV.UK guidance says MTD for Income Tax is mandatory from 6 April 2026 for qualifying income above £50,000, then £30,000 from 6 April 2027 and £20,000 from 6 April 2028. HMRC's February 2026 news page also said more than 860,000 sole traders and landlords needed to start using digital reporting, so this is active compliance demand rather than a speculative trend.
top_opportunities.buyer: Sole traders, portfolio landlords, independent bookkeepers and small accountancy practices
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: High - setup fee plus monthly or quarterly support
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Excellent for a solo engineer because the first offer is migration, workflow setup and dashboarding, not building a full accounting platform.
risks: Accounting software vendors already own the core ledger, so the wedge must be onboarding, cleanup and admin reduction
Stay away from regulated tax advice and position the offer as implementation and process automation
top_opportunities.verdict: Best overall because the trigger is real, time-bounded and service-first, with a clean path into recurring support.

top_opportunities.name: Invoice chasing and e-invoicing automation for UK SMEs
top_opportunities.description: Build a productized service or lightweight SaaS that issues cleaner invoices, automates reminders, tracks promises to pay and gives owners a simple overdue-cash dashboard.
top_opportunities.why_now: Evidence is strong. A March 2026 GOV.UK announcement said late payments cost the UK economy £11 billion per year, 38 businesses close every day because they are not paid on time, and government plans include a 60-day cap on payment terms for large firms plus mandatory late-payment interest. The Small Business Commissioner's e-invoicing guidance also says moving to e-invoicing can reduce invoicing costs by 60 to 80 percent and speed payment.
top_opportunities.buyer: Micro businesses, agencies, consultancies, trades firms and other B2B service SMEs
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: High - monthly subscription or managed receivables retainer
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Very strong for a solo engineer because this is mainly reminders, integrations, workflow logic and reporting.
risks: Some buyers will assume their accounting package already solves this unless you frame the pitch around debtor days and cash collection
Messy data across Xero, QuickBooks and spreadsheet-led workflows can slow onboarding
top_opportunities.verdict: One of the best immediate-cash opportunities because owners feel the pain now and the ROI story is obvious.

top_opportunities.name: AI lead-response and admin automation for trades and local service firms
top_opportunities.description: Productize missed-call text-back, instant form replies, quote follow-up, booking handoff and a simple pipeline dashboard for firms that lose jobs through slow response times.
top_opportunities.why_now: Evidence is solid but less direct than compliance or invoicing. The government's AI Opportunities Action Plan explicitly says it wants to ramp up AI adoption across the UK to boost growth and productivity, which supports practical automation offers. The opportunity is strongest when sold as faster lead handling and fewer admin hours, not generic AI consulting.
top_opportunities.buyer: Plumbers, electricians, HVAC firms, clinics, salons, cleaning firms and other local service operators
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Strong for a solo engineer because the stack is mostly messaging, CRM glue, forms, scheduling and reporting.
risks: Competition is visible, so a narrow niche and hard ROI framing are essential
Some local firms lack clean lead data, which makes proof of value harder until tracking is installed
top_opportunities.verdict: Strong if packaged around recovered revenue and saved admin time rather than broad AI transformation language.

top_opportunities.name: Tender monitoring and bid-drafting assistant for SME suppliers
top_opportunities.description: Create a niche service or tool that monitors relevant tenders, scores fit, drafts first-pass answers and stores reusable evidence for small suppliers selling to government.
top_opportunities.why_now: Evidence is credible. GOV.UK says the Procurement Act 2023 has been in force since 24 February 2025, with nearly £400 billion of yearly public spend, simpler bidding processes, more standardisation and streamlining, and greater access for small businesses. That creates room for a focused monitoring and drafting layer, though sales cycles can be slower than tax or cashflow services.
top_opportunities.buyer: Small consultancies, digital agencies, specialist service firms and compliance-heavy B2B suppliers
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: Medium to high - retainer, seat fee or managed bid support
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Good for an engineer who can combine tender scraping, search, document generation and workflow tooling into a narrow offer.
risks: Tendering is episodic, so revenue can be lumpier than in compliance services
Bid-writing agencies already exist, so you need automation leverage or a tight vertical niche
top_opportunities.verdict: Promising niche B2B play, best when aimed at one supplier category instead of the whole procurement market.

top_opportunities.name: Cyber Essentials readiness and small-business security workflow automation
top_opportunities.description: Offer a fixed-scope package covering backups, MFA, account hygiene, device checks, phishing prompts and evidence collection for small organisations that need a better baseline.
top_opportunities.why_now: Evidence is real but slightly softer on fastest buying urgency. The NCSC small-organisations guide says there are 5.5 million UK small organisations with 0 to 49 employees, that 1 in 2 small businesses suffer a cyber incident every year, and that many protective steps can be done quickly. That supports a checklist-driven, productized offer rather than bespoke security consulting.
top_opportunities.buyer: Small professional firms, charities, clinics, agencies and B2B service SMEs
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: Medium to high - monitoring, policy reviews and periodic checks
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Good if you prefer structured technical delivery and can keep the service standardized.
risks: Trust matters in security work, so some buyers will prefer established specialists
If the offer becomes too bespoke, part-time solo delivery gets much harder
top_opportunities.verdict: Viable as a productized operations package, but less immediate than the top two opportunities.

best_pick.name: Making Tax Digital onboarding and quarterly-support automation
best_pick.why_it_wins: It has the clearest live trigger, fits a solo engineer on a low budget, can be sold as a service before any software build-out, and naturally converts into recurring quarterly support or bookkeeping-adjacent admin retainers.
validation_plan: Pick one wedge first: landlords with simple property records or small accountancy firms that need extra MTD onboarding capacity.
Build a tiny demo showing record capture, reminder workflows and a client-status dashboard on top of off-the-shelf accounting software.
Publish a fixed-price MTD readiness package with clear scope, turnaround time and a monthly or quarterly support option.
Contact 30 to 50 local accountants, bookkeepers and landlord-focused businesses before writing custom software beyond dashboards and automation.
best_pick.first_offer: MTD readiness setup for sole traders and landlords: records cleanup, software connection, reminder workflows and first-quarter support. Charge roughly £299 to £799 setup plus £39 to £149 per month depending on complexity.
best_pick.mvp: A thin internal tool plus off-the-shelf bookkeeping software, document capture, reminder automation and a simple dashboard for onboarding status and quarterly check-ins.
customer_types: Sole traders above the current MTD threshold
best_pick.do_today: Create a one-page MTD readiness offer, build a dashboard mock-up and start direct outreach to local accountants and landlord-facing businesses today.
